commit.message:
Rebalance beat reactivity: visible but natural

Previous commit went too subtle — effects were nearly invisible.
Restore noticeable glow/pulse while keeping the slower, smoother
transitions and higher beat threshold from the previous fix.

Middle ground between the original electric flicker and invisible:
- Lyrics glow: 14px max (was 10, orig 18), 0.2s transition
- Scale: 1.2% (was 0.8%, orig 1.5%)
- Word pulse: 1.06 scale (was 1.04, orig 1.08)
- Phone frame: 16px glow (was 12, orig 20)
- Zone2: restore full beatPulse pass-through to text glow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Feature/cron suspension lifecycle (#12)

* fix: disable cron jobs on trial expiry/payment lapse, re-enable on reactivation

Three-layer fix for cron messages leaking to suspended tenants:

1. CronDeliveryView: blocks delivery for non-ACTIVE tenants (returns 200
   to prevent QStash retries). Belt-and-suspenders defense.

2. expire_trials + handle_invoice_payment_failed: disable all cron jobs
   via gateway BEFORE hibernating container. Jobs are disabled (not
   deleted) so they can be restored on reactivation.

3. handle_checkout_completed: re-enables all disabled cron jobs when a
   suspended tenant subscribes and their container wakes up.

New module: apps/cron/suspension.py
- suspend_tenant_crons(): disables all enabled cron jobs
- resume_tenant_crons(): re-enables all disabled cron jobs
- Both are idempotent and handle partial failures gracefully

12 new tests covering suspend/resume/block lifecycle.

* fix: update suspended tenant tests to match 'paused' copy

Tests were asserting 'trial has ended' but the user-facing copy was
changed to 'assistant is paused' in a573951. Aligns test assertions
with current copy.

* fix: use web_fetch instead of curl for weather in briefing prompt

Subscriber agents have group:web (web_fetch, web_search) but NOT exec.
The morning briefing prompt was telling them to use 'curl' which they
can't run — causing hallucinated weather data.

Now explicitly instructs: use web_fetch tool, do NOT use curl/exec.

* fix: switch weather source to Open-Meteo API (web_fetch compatible)

wttr.in via web_fetch is unreliable — JSON format fails, special char
formats fail, 15-25s latency on simple queries.

Open-Meteo works perfectly: structured JSON, 3s response, free, no auth.
Prompt now includes WMO weather code legend so agent can interpret.

* feat: template weather URL with tenant coordinates into briefing prompt

Instead of telling the agent to 'build the URL with the user's
coordinates' (which it can't do without knowing them), we now:

1. Map IANA timezone → approximate lat/lon (major city per timezone)
2. Build the full Open-Meteo URL server-side
3. Template it directly into the morning briefing prompt

The agent just needs to call web_fetch with the pre-built URL.
No guessing coordinates, no hallucinating weather data.

New module: apps/orchestrator/weather.py
- 50+ timezone→coords mappings
- build_weather_url(tz) → ready-to-fetch URL
- get_coords_for_timezone(tz) with region-based fallbacks

* feat: add user location fields for accurate weather

Simplest-first approach:

1. User model: added location_city, location_lat, location_lon fields
2. Runtime profile endpoint: accepts location fields via PATCH
3. Plugin tool (nbhd_update_profile): added location params
4. Weather URL builder: prefers stored coords, falls back to timezone
5. AGENTS.md template: tells agent to ask for city if not set

Flow: agent asks user their city → looks up coords via web_search →
calls nbhd_update_profile → config refreshes → next morning briefing
uses real coordinates.

Migration: 0022_add_user_location_fields

* feat: full location stack — serializer, settings UI, runtime endpoint, onboarding, tests

Complete location feature buildout:

Backend:
- UserSerializer: exposes location_city, location_lat, location_lon
- ProfileView (user-facing PATCH): accepts location fields, triggers
  config refresh on change
- RuntimeProfileUpdateView (agent-facing PATCH): accepts location fields
  with coordinate validation (-90..90 lat, -180..180 lon)
- Onboarding USER.md: includes location if set

Frontend:
- Settings page: Location card with city input, edit/save/cancel flow
- API client: updated updateProfile type signature
- Copy: 'Not set — used for weather and local info' placeholder

Agent:
- AGENTS.md template: tells agent to ask for city if not set, look up
  coords via web_search, call nbhd_update_profile
- Plugin tool (nbhd_update_profile): accepts location_city, location_lat,
  location_lon params

Tests:
- 4 ProfileLocationAPITest: GET includes fields, PATCH city, PATCH with
  coords, config refresh on change
- 4 RuntimeProfileLocationTest: set city, set with coords, invalid
  coords 400, response includes fields

* fix: add location fields to AuthUser type + polish

- Add location_city, location_lat, location_lon to AuthUser interface
  (fixes frontend-test CI failure)
- Add null guard, autoFocus, maxLength to location input
- Add lat/lon range validators to UserSerializer

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add AI-driven UI cards: request_user_input tool for smart form collection

New architecture: AI can trigger interactive UI cards (forms, confirms, choices)
inline in chat via request_user_input tool. Cards render with prefilled data +
editable fields. User fills in and clicks Gem — data merges and saves to profile.

- tools.py: New request_user_input tool with ui_type (form/confirm/choice),
  prefilled data, configurable fields, and save_action
- agent.py: Buffer ui_card events, stream after text for natural flow order.
  Updated system prompt with examples of when to use request_user_input vs
  update_user_profile
- index.html: New ui_card SSE handler renders forms with tags, inputs, and
  submit button. _submitUiCard merges prefilled + field values and POSTs
- __init__.py: Added update_summary action to confirm endpoint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Agregar módulos ERP, testing y corregir bugs de seguridad y dashboard

Módulos backend nuevos:
- budget-versions: presupuesto versionado con coeficiente K
- certificates: certificaciones con máquina de estados y deducciones
- adjustments: redeterminación de precios (índices, fórmulas, cálculo)
- price-analysis: análisis de precios unitarios (APU)
- progress: avance físico por ítem
- subcontracts: subcontrataciones
- currencies: multi-moneda y tipos de cambio
- labor-categories, equipment-catalog, financial-plans

Frontend: páginas y formularios para todos los módulos nuevos,
componentes UI (tabs, progress, collapsible) y charts (S-curve)

Testing:
- Infraestructura Vitest (unit + integration configs, CI workflow)
- 146 tests unitarios: validators argentinos, RBAC, html-escape,
  budget-versions service, certificate calculations, adjustment factor
- Tests de integración: auth (26 tests) y projects (18 tests)
  con helpers de setup/teardown y aislamiento por organización

Seguridad (fixes):
- XSS en reportes HTML: aplicar escapeHtml() en reports.routes.ts
- IDOR en employees: verificar org antes de exponer proyectos/asistencia

Dashboard (fixes):
- Respuesta del API tenía estructura plana; frontend esperaba { kpis, projectCards }
- Campos calculados (overdueTasksCount, lowStockCount, etc.) no se incluían en sendSuccess
- projectCards y activeProjects filtraban solo IN_PROGRESS; ahora incluyen PLANNING
- Excluir src/__tests__ del tsconfig de producción para evitar errores en Docker build

commit.message:
feat: reintroduce airflow with calibration formula

Raw value at index 14 is converted to m³/h using:
  if raw > 200: flow = (raw - 700) / 30 + 20, rounded to 1 decimal
Example: 1025 → 30.8 m³/h

commit.message:
docs: add PostgREST HTTP 300 function overload learning

When a migration creates a new RPC function with different parameter types
without dropping the old version, PostgREST can't disambiguate and returns
HTTP 300. Always drop all overloads before recreating.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Optimize solver with constraint propagation and MRV heuristic

Replace naive backtracking with bitset-based constraint propagation
(naked singles + hidden singles) and minimum remaining values cell
selection for significantly faster solution counting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hearth: Ready to Merge panel with merge action (Forge-npl) (#86)

* feat: Hearth Ready to Merge panel with merge action

Add a new panel to the Hearth TUI that lists PRs ready to merge.
A PR is ready when: CI checks pass, has approval (status=approved),
no merge conflicts, and no unresolved review threads.

Changes:
- State DB: Add is_conflicting and has_unresolved_threads columns to
  prs table; add ReadyToMergePRs() query and UpdatePRMergeability()
- Bellows: Persist conflict/unresolved thread state on each poll cycle
- GhPR: Add Merge() function (gh pr merge with configurable strategy)
- IPC: Add merge_pr command with MergePRPayload
- Daemon: Handle merge_pr command, use config merge_strategy setting
- Config: Add MergeStrategy setting (default: squash)
- Hearth: Add PanelReadyToMerge in left column between Queue and
  Needs Attention, with Enter to open merge action menu overlay
- cmd/forge/hearth: Wire OnMergePR callback via IPC

The panel is reactive — PRs disappear when bellows detects state
changes (new comments, conflicts, CI failures, approval dismissed)
because the DB columns are updated on each bellows poll.

Bead: Forge-npl

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Return ReadyToMergeErrorMsg on DB error instead of silently clearing panel
- Handle ReadyToMergeErrorMsg in hearth Update to surface errors in event log
- Add unit tests for renderReadyToMerge (empty, items, selection, merge menu, error msg)

  success; removes misleading post-merge "requested" log — Comment 7

commit.message:
fix: grant contents write permission to flake update workflow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix sourceHealth statement timeout: only persist non-default entries

The sourceHealth JSON blob (~8KB for 42 sources) was hitting Supabase's
statement timeout on cross-region INSERT/UPDATE. Strip healthy sources
(0 failures, no error) before writing — they default to healthy on read.
Reduces payload from ~8KB to a few hundred bytes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix WASD keys moving character while typing in input fields

Skip the yard key handler when an input or textarea element has focus,
so the player can type names (and other text) without triggering movement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: SSR meta injection, bundle optimization, CI/CD, security

SSR META INJECTION (biggest SEO impact):
- Server injects title, description, og:*, canonical, JSON-LD into HTML
- Static routes: cached in memory, zero DB queries
- Dynamic routes (blog/zones): DB query with fallback
- Google now sees full SEO signals without executing JavaScript

BUNDLE OPTIMIZATION:
- BlogPost: 128KB → 12.84KB (-90%) by replacing react-markdown with inline converter
- Main index: 188KB → 133KB (-29%) by splitting i18n into separate chunk
- Static assets: Cache-Control 1 year + immutable headers

GITHUB ACTIONS:
- CI workflow: type-check + build on push/PRs
- Lighthouse CI: performance/a11y/SEO budgets on 3 key pages

SECURITY:
- Content-Security-Policy header added
- Session cookie secure=true in production
- CSP allows Google Analytics, fonts, maps, WhatsApp

commit.message:
fix: support legacy KV_REST_API_URL env var names for Upstash Redis

Vercel's Upstash integration injects KV_REST_API_URL / KV_REST_API_TOKEN
instead of the expected UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL / UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN.
Resolve both names at module load time so the cache works regardless of
which naming convention Vercel used. Also adds a startup log line to make
it easy to confirm the backend is active in Vercel function logs.

commit.message:
Fix SetForegroundWindow silently failing without AttachThreadInput

SetForegroundWindow is restricted by Windows — it fails when the calling
process doesn't own the current foreground window. Use AttachThreadInput
to temporarily attach to the foreground thread before switching focus.
Without this fix, HID keystrokes went to the terminal instead of the
Windows Security dialog.

- Confetti演出: パーフェクト時に紙吹雪アニメーション
- 新しい星獲得時のバウンスアニメーション
- ホーム画面に星とハイスコア表示
- 記録画面にデータ初期化ボタン追加

fix(analytics): first_date falls back to vehicle_crossings when traffic_daily is empty

traffic_daily is only populated by the nightly aggregation job. On a fresh
deployment it's empty so first_date was always null. Fall back to the oldest
vehicle_crossings row so the Recording Since notice renders correctly.

feat: report setters return true only when data changed (#5)

* feat: report setters return true only when report data changed

Button, hat, and axis setters now compare old vs new values and return
true only if the report buffer was actually modified. This allows callers
to skip sending unchanged reports.

commit.message:
Add Cath's defense evaluation vs all SOTA attacks

- New experiment: experiments/cath_defense_sota_test.py tests 8 sanitizers
  and 4 anomaly detectors (C. Wang) against PatchSteg, PCA-PatchSteg,
  PSyDUCK-inspired, and CDF-PatchSteg at 128px
- Key findings: Entropy MAD / Spectral KL detect all ±ε attacks (AUC 1.0)
  but are blind to CDF-PatchSteg (AUC 0.11-0.56). Latent Smooth σ=1.0 is
  the strongest sanitizer but only reduces accuracy to 68-83%
- Added figures: cath_defense_heatmap.png, cath_sanitize_all_attacks.png
- Added Section (sec:cath_defenses) to paper with sanitizer and detector
  tables and heatmap figure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
